review_title: Gusto is awesome! Best HR Software there is
description: I have loved Gusto. I've used almost all of the big payroll/HR systems and Gusto is head and shoulders above the rest. Easy to use, awesome automations, great at gathering feedback, makes sense, and more.
author: Greg R.
pros: Gusto is awesome! Easiest HR system I've used. Super simple and straightforward. Has great workflows. Is so easy to understand and access as a user.
cons: I honestly don't have any complaints about Gusto. I've had a couple of login issues before, but nothing big stands out.

review_title: Great payroll tool for small businesses. Way better than ADP
description: Overall, Gusto has been a reliable and time-saving solution for managing payroll and HR for our small business. The setup was straightforward and the ongoing maintenance is minimal. While there are some areas for improvement, particularly around pricing and support, it covers the essentials well and has noticeably reduced the stress of payroll processing.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Gusto makes payroll incredibly easy to run — even for someone without a dedicated HR or accounting background. The interface is clean and intuitive, and the automated tax filing saves a significant amount of time. The onboarding flow for new employees is smooth and largely self-service, which reduces the administrative burden. Integration with popular accounting tools like QuickBooks is also a big plus.
cons: The pricing can add up quickly as you scale, especially when adding features like time tracking or benefits administration. Customer support response times can be slow during peak periods. Some advanced reporting features feel limited compared to dedicated HR platforms, and the mobile app could use more functionality for managers.

author: Sophie B.
pros: Being in HR, using a platform like Gusto was very helpful as everything is one place. I found it quite easy to process payroll and onboard new employees. I only have 3 locations that I had to cover but it made it easy to switch between different locations. As well as offering the different ways to label the employees and make sure the correct tax documents were associated with the correct person.
cons: I felt as though anytime I needed something through customer support, it would be someone who does not truly understand the situation or they did not speak english. I had no issues with time management and getting them on the phones as they would call right away but even with that, sometimes I did not get my issues resolved

review_title: Great payroll system for small businesses and nonprofits
description: We have been using Gusto for payroll for several years now and it has made our payroll process very easy. We are a small nonprofit with only a handful of paid employees, but they work from home in multiple states and Gusto made that all simple to setup and manage.
author: John B.
pros: Gusto is straightforward and easy to set up, provides simple-to-use tools for running payroll, and is one of the least expensive solutions available to small businesses.
cons: It was tricky to get Gusto fully integrated with our preferred timekeeping system. Additionally, user roles and permissions cannot be customized, so you must choose from a small set of pre-configured roles - but these were sufficient for our use.

review_title: A Reliable and User-Friendly Payroll Solution
description: The overall experience with Gusto has been great. The platform is user-friendly, reliable, and makes managing payroll, benefits, and HR tasks much simpler.
author: Lynne P.
pros: What I like most about Gusto is how it simplifies payroll and HR tasks. It's user friendly, automates time consuming processes, and helps ensure employees are paid accurately and on time.
cons: There's not much I dislike about Gusto. The only small challenge is that new users may need a little time to get familiar with all the features, but once you do, it's very intuitive.

author: Cecelia B.
pros: Very easy to use. The app is very easy to navigate through. The website is also easy to navigate through. I only had one bad experience with Gusto, but they fixed the bugs and issues really quick, and their customer support helped out as well.
cons: Sometimes Gusto takes a bit to load the screens. It's isn't terribly slow but it does slow down the process a bit.

review_title: Effortless Payroll and HR Made Easy
description: My overall experience with Gusto has been positive. The platform is easy to use and does a great job simplifying payroll and HR tasks that can otherwise be time-consuming and complicated. I especially appreciated the automation features, which made processes like tax filings and employee payments much more efficient. While there are some limitations with customization and pricing for more advanced features, the convenience and reliability of the system make it a strong option for small to mid-sized businesses.
author: Yasmin M.
pros: What I liked most about Gusto was how user-friendly and intuitive the platform is. Everything from running payroll to managing employee information is clearly organized, which makes it easy to navigate even without much prior experience. I also appreciated how automated many processes are, like tax calculations and filings, which saves a lot of time and reduces the risk of errors. Overall, it simplifies complex HR and payroll tasks in a way that feels efficient and stress-free.
cons: What I liked least about Gusto is that some of its features can feel limited, especially for more complex business needs. While it works well for basic payroll and HR tasks, customization options aren’t as flexible as they could be.

review_title: A payroll and benefits tool that makes me smile!
description: It's awesome - I truly enjoy checking my pay or benefits with their system, so that's a huge win in my book.
author: Michael H.
pros: Gusto is such an awesome HR and benefits product! I love the vibe in the UI - they've made lots of things - pay, benefits - super super easy to access and update.
cons: Maybe just that there's a lot more visible to me than I need to access - lots of other features that aren't really helpful and clutter up the view a bit.

review_title: A Nonprofit’s Dream: Payroll and HR Made Simple
description: Unlike other platforms that require a week of setup, Gusto was easy to dive into immediately. It’s the most user-friendly HR and payroll tool I’ve encountered. It has streamlined our nonprofit's workflow perfectly, making it easy to manage a mixed team of staff and contractors without any of the usual headaches
author: Jennifer W.
pros: Everything. Gusto has made life as a small nonprofit manageable in so many ways. From onboarding contractors and staff to seamless time-tracking, it has turned what used to be a massive administrative burden into a simple, automated process.
cons: Honestly, I can’t think of a single thing I dislike about Gusto. It has met every need our nonprofit has without any of the typical software headaches.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is definitely a product that provides good value for the money. I've been using it for a couple of years now and it works great. They provide regular updates and keep you informed as to when things need to be done.
cons: Sometimes it can be a challenge getting ahold of anyone to help since it's all online. The length of the process make take a bit longer.

description: I'd recommend Gusto to even the smallest of businesses. It simplifies Payroll and Human Resources, it bundles a lot of useful tools such as onboarding, and its scalable.
author: Raylon H.
pros: Gusto is relativley easy to use. Its intuitive interface is very user friendly granting forward access to setting up and managing payroll, onboarding, and benefits.
cons: Customer support with Gusto can be slow and sometimes not helpful. The mobile app is also not very inclusive, you have to log in on a web based computer to effectively manage.

description: It was okay. Not the best, but it serves its intended function. Price-wise, I think they are competitive enough for the offerings. I would recommend it for small businesses that need to pay US-based staff. For international staff, there's some limitations on countries you can enroll.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Cheap option for paying US-based contractors and employees. Their customer support is also great. Every time I request support, someone is available online or over the phone to talk to, which is a feature that most other similar providers lack.
cons: The user interface could be challenging. There is definitely a learning stage for new users who have never used similar platforms before. In my experience, I've used several platforms similar to Gusto, and I believe their payroll function needs improvement. There are limitations on reporting fuctions that needs some customization.

description: Poor. I expect so much more from such a prominent software. I also expect 24/7 human tech support when dealing with payroll for companies that have all sorts of hours of operation. We are restaurants and are operating non-stop in many ways.
author: Todd R.
pros: Much better interface than Quickbooks. Attempts to integrate a hiring module that does present an easy, online application form.
cons: 1. Gusto missed one of our paydays. Gusto withdrew the funds, and deposits did not arrive on the following day as promised. You can imagine the impact on our 45 employees and their level of trust in our company. Hours of corresponding with their support team both by chat and phone left no resolution. Gusto does not have a human support network for after 7pm CST. 2. User interface is clunky. They still have the "25 records" is the total records listed at the bottom of the page and you have to choose 50, or 100 every single time you want to see a whole list. 3. You cannot assign employees to multiple departments within the same location. 4. Alerts come up on the home screen and you cannot easily make them go away. 5. The hiring module does not have a way of managing one applicant who has applied for multiple jobs. The system sees it all as separate applicant records.

review_title: Gusto does what's needed to make paying people straight forward.
description: Overall I have been happy with Gusto. There is a live person you can get ahold of when needed. They are constantly improving their platform to make things easier for the busy business owner that is doing it all. Some highlights are the time tracking and scheduling. Some complaints I get from my team is that the kiosk doesn't clock you out. I think that if you don't hit the buttons just right your entry isn't processed.
author: Kim J.
pros: Gusto does it all and they have great customer service. I have called many times and typically my problems are fixed within a few minutes. They tend to have good follow through with issues. They are committed to improving their platform regularly. This is helpful. I was glad they've added time tracking for contractors. This makes paying people straight forward.
cons: Not a huge fan of their al-a-cart pricing. It is quite a bit extra per month for HR services. Most of which you can google on your own. I think they could benefit from asking long term customers for feedback.

description: It's a 75% application. It's 75% great, but there are few issues that could be resolved to make it a 100% great application. It's worth the money for a smaller business, but for larger enterprises, there are better solutions.
author: Eric T.
pros: Ease of use. From a user perspective it is convenient with a very simple UI. Navigation is intuitive and provides an excellent user experience. The mobile app provides a very similar UX.
cons: The only part of the UI that doesn't make a lot of sense is the time edit save processes. When time is edited with a note added, the user can't hit "Enter" to save the record update. In fact, clicking "Enter" will close the window without the update. In most applications "Enter" will result in the submission.

description: Great service, would highly recommend, great pricing. It is easy and intuitive. I appreciate getting the reminders to run payroll.
author: Grant K.
pros: Fast and precise payroll. Direct deposit is great. Taxes are paid on time with no missed deadlines or penalties.
cons: Inability to customize withholding taxes while running payroll. You do have to go to the individual employee and change their withholdings.

review_title: Gusto is a great software! Highly Suggest!
description: Overall, Gusto is an excellent resource for payroll, managing salaries, and tracking time off. We also find it very useful for hosting employee manuals, documents, and training modules. Our team completes their annual sexual harassment training through the platform, which is quick and engaging thanks to the interactive videos! We have been using Gusto for years and it has truly changed how we operate. While we have a relatively small team, I can imagine this software would be equally beneficial for a larger organization. Plus, the seasonal outfits on the loading page mascot (a cute little pig) are a delightful touch! We love it!
author: Sara P.
pros: Gusto has significantly streamlined our payroll and time tracking. Its user-friendly interface makes it easy to monitor employee hours and resolve issues efficiently. The location tracking feature is particularly helpful, as it allows employees to manually clock in, clock out, and take breaks within our building's geofence all from their phones!
cons: The user interface for employees can be somewhat clunky at times. They have reported long loading periods when attempting to clock in or out, and there are occasional issues with the location [tracking/accuracy].

description: We love it and have been using it for over 10 years. I highly recommend anyone who has to do payroll to use it. Takes the headache out of payroll and HR.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Works really well and is easy to navigate. The onboarding and benefits are streamlined and easy for everyone. My teammates love the app and that they can see everything in one place.
cons: The customer service can be a little frustrating at times. Sometimes you get someone who understands and is knowledgeable. Sometimes you don't.

review_title: Excellent for payroll. Mediocre for everything else.
description: Gusto works best as a payroll-focused checking account, not as your financial command center. It excels at getting you paid reliably, often early, and without confusion. If that’s your main goal, it delivers. If you want robust banking tools or fast individual support, you’ll likely need a second bank alongside it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Consistent, on-time payroll deposits. Paychecks arrive when they’re supposed to, which is the bare minimum but still worth praising. Early direct deposit. Funds often show up up to two days early, which actually helps instead of just sounding helpful. Simple, clean interface. Pay stubs, tax documents, and transaction history are easy to find and easy to read. No hidden fees. No monthly maintenance fees or surprise deductions quietly stealing lunch money. Clear pay breakdowns. Earnings, deductions, and year-to-date totals are transparent and straightforward.
cons: Limited banking features. This is a checking account, not a full-service bank. No joint accounts, limited bill pay, and minimal extras. Support favors employers. If you’re just an employee with a problem, response times can feel slower than they should. Cash deposits are inconvenient. Not ideal if you still live in the physical-currency world. Few customization options. Alerts, budgeting tools, and spending controls are pretty basic.

review_title: Consistent, reliable, and easy to use
description: Overall, Gusto is the most cost effective option to manage all things employees. I've never experienced any bugs or outages that affected my business in the 10 years or so I've used it.
author: Abbey W.
pros: I love how simple it is to use as Gusto guides you through all the requirements, from taxes to reports and files them all automatically.
cons: It's a bit difficult to link Gusto to benefits like workers comp and insurance. You have to deal with a few different reps to get it to completed.

description: The best way to describe it is easy. Everting is stored for you digitally so you don’t have to keep track of documents. I love the app. It’s easy to use and user friendly.
author: Hayleigh B.
pros: How easy it is to track your hours,time off and pay. I can go back to the first paycheck I got. I can also request time off and see my balances in an instant.
cons: Gusto requires me to put a note every time I clock in and it doesn’t any of my coworkers. I just type “on site” each morning because I don’t know what else to say.

description: I overall loved gusto and being able to access my 401k on there! Overall the account management was easy to use as well as accessing data on Gusto.
author: Michael D.
pros: I loved being able to access my 401k and see my monthly payment breakdowns! It was easy to use and as an employee it was easy to read.
cons: I dont think there was anything in my time using gusto that was bad. There were no bugs or issues that occur when I was using the app!

author: Kearis O.
pros: When the system worked, it worked well and had good functionality. I would say its biggest strength is the variety of reports and customization available.
cons: We often had the platform go down at random times, and the support team was not very helpful. For instance, the card we had on file to pay our subscription expired, so the whole system went down. No one contacted us to let us know that was the problem, or we could have solved it before losing access to the system. Also, Gusto lacked the scheduling and employee communication tools that we found in another system.

description: Overall my experience with Gusto was excellent. It provided convince and was simple to integrate with different jobs.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What i liked most about Gusto was that you have everything all in one. I was able to clock into work and have my direct deposit sent right to my gusto account.
cons: What I liked least about Gusto is that it can be picky sometimes when trying to sign into my account.

description: Positive and gets the job done! I would recommend this as a dedicated time tracker for any small business looking for such a service. Its dependable and reliable!
author: Tahja P.
pros: Ease of access and how simplified and easy to understand the menus are, it is easy to "punch" and clock in and also have employees times adjusted.
cons: A few bugs where it wasn't loading for a few seconds, but these bugs were quickly ironed out and We haven't had any other issues since.
